revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 魚沼神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|300px]] |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]土川2丁目699-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|18|18.0|N|138|47|15.8|E|region:JP-15_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=魚沼神社}} |祭神 = 天香語山命 |社格 = 式内社論社・伝越後国二宮・旧県社 |創建 = 伝崇神天皇朝 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = 上弥彦大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月7日]] |神事 = }}
'''魚沼神社'''（うおぬまじんじゃ）は[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]土川2丁目にある[[神社]]である。境内には、国の[[重要文化財]]の阿弥陀堂がある。
==祭神== *[[天香語山命]] *[[豊玉姫命]] *阿彦
==歴史== 創建は[[崇神天皇]]の時代と伝えられている。魚沼神社という名は[[平安時代]]からあり、『[[延喜式神名帳|延喜神名式]]』にも[[魚沼郡]]の神社5社が記され、その中に「魚沼神社」もあるが、当神社と一致するか否かについては[[江戸時代]]以来諸説がある。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には、[[彌彦神社]]（[[弥彦村]]）と祭神が同じことから「上弥彦神社」と呼ばれ、[[上杉謙信]]の時代の史料にも「上弥彦大明神」とあり、拝殿にも「彌彦大明神」の額が掲げられている。[[一宮|越後国一宮]]である弥彦村の彌彦神社に対し、当神社を「二の宮」と呼び、この地域の人々の[[信仰]]の中心となった。「魚沼神社」と称したのは[[幕末]]頃で、[[式内社]]であるという確証はない。現在の土川に移る前は上ノ山にあった。
*[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）-魚沼神社が[[従五位下]]の[[神階]]を賜う。 *[[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）-[[新田義貞]]挙兵に伴い魚沼神社神官挙げて従う。 *[[延元]]2年（[[1337年]]）-新田義貞敗れ、従軍した神官は散り、[[慈眼寺]]･円明寺は[[五智院 (小千谷市)|五智院]]末寺となる。 *[[永享]]9年（[[1437年]]）-11月15日、[[西片弥三郎光行]]が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[明応]]元年（[[1492年]]）-西片弥三郎光行が[[懸仏]]4体寄進。 *[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）-9月13日、上杉謙信から社領100貫と魚沼神社18末社に各御供田一町半反を寄進。 *同年-11月28日、上杉謙信[[関東]]出陣の際、魚沼神社に宿泊。 *同12年（[[1569年]]）-[[越中国]][[砺波郡]][[糸岡庄]]七社明神から手写『[[大般若経]]』を魚沼神社へ寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）-神像（現存11体）作成。 *同8年（[[1580年]]）-前々の如く諸役停止し、社領安堵。 *同9年（[[1581年]]）-[[上杉景勝]]から修造料に充てる為に、河舟1艘、関銭免除。 *[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）-玉橋･垣製作 *[[慶長]]3年（[[1598年]]）-[[堀氏]]により、社領を802[[石 (単位)|石]]から10石に削減。 *[[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）-[[朱印地]]20石で紛争。魚沼神社側が有利に決着する。 *[[貞享]]4年（[[1687年]]）-[[上田銀山]]の[[高田藩]]主献上分を魚沼神社で祈祷した上で献上するようになる。 *[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）-[[郷社]]に列し、後に[[県社]]となった。
==境内== [[鳥居]]は、[[両部鳥居]]。[[手水舎]]の隣には、小川の横に御手洗場と見られる石段がある。[[拝殿]]に向かって左側に[[阿弥陀堂]]、右側に[[神楽殿]]、右斜め前には日露戦役記念碑が建てられている。[[本殿]]の脇には、神輿舎、祠がある。
;阿弥陀堂 :[[室町時代]]末期[[永禄]]6年（[[1563年]]）頃の建立で、国の重要文化財に指定されている。方三間、[[宝形造]][[茅葺]]の小堂である。堂内には鍛冶屋が[[信濃川]]の[[砂鉄]]でつくったといわれる[[阿弥陀如来]]像と[[大日如来]]像が安置される。[[昭和]]29年（[[1954年]]）に解体修理が行われ、後世の改変部分は建立当初の形態に復元された。
== 境外末社 == 主に茶郷川流域に18末社がある。 *土川村（現土川） **所謂、天王社 *小千谷町（現本町･元町･平成など） **二荒社 **山王社 **赤指社 *時水村（現時水） **俣倉社 **熊野社 **石動社 **十二社 *藪川村（現藪川） **宇都宮神社 ***永享10年（[[1438年]]）7月19日に西片弥三郎光行が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 **諏訪神社 *吉谷村（現吉谷） **白山社 **五霊社 **若宮社 **十二社 **八幡社 **白山社 *四ツ子村（現四ツ子） **俣倉社 *谷内村（現谷内） **熊野社
==伝説== ===上杉謙信と神軍=== 上杉謙信が[[関東]]へ出陣する際は、[[小千谷市|小千谷]]で[[信濃川]]を渡っていた。その小千谷を通るたびに、この魚沼神社に参りに来ていた。ある時、[[社殿]]で戦勝祈願の[[儀式]]を済ませて[[拝殿]]に立つと、遥か彼方に[[天香語山命]]と18末社の神々が関東に向かっている姿が見えた。関東の戦いの最中にも神の御旗が現れ、大勝利を抑えた。[[越後国]]に帰ると、謙信は本社に100余貫文の年貢の上がる土地、18末社にもそれぞれ1町8反の土地を寄進した。 ===神に救われた兵=== [[第二次世界大戦]]中、中国大陸を転戦している軍曹がいた。暗闇の夜に、部隊の一番後ろを歩いていると、疲れでよろめいた拍子に道脇の空井戸に落ちてしまった。すると、上から明かりが射し、白い着物の白髪の老人が現れ、提灯を掲げ、縄を下ろされた。井戸から出ると、老人は「ついて来なさい」と言い、歩き出した。道を照らす提灯には「上弥彦神社」と書かれていた。部隊が居る所に着くと、老人はいなくなっていた。 ===婆っ貝=== 信心深いお婆さんがいた。親類の婆さんが長患いで寝ているのを気の毒に、毎日、魚沼神社へ早く治るように祈りに来ていた。ある夜、夢に白装束の神が現れ、「毎日、神社の後ろの田から婆っ貝（[[カラスガイ]]のこと）を採り、親類の婆さんに食べさせれば病気は治る」と言った。次の日から婆っ貝を採って親類の婆さんに届けた。すると、一月ほどで婆さんは病気が治り、前よりも元気になった。 ===大木様=== 神社の倉庫の裏に「大木さま」という大きな[[杉]]の木があった。老木になり、嵐で倒れてしまうと、木の先が上ノ山の麓まで届き橋の様になった。その木で神主は神像を作った。神社の裏にも「大木さま」に負けないほどの大木があった。この木が倒れて、先がとどいた所はいまでも小字で杉崎という地名で残っている。 ===一杯清水=== [[善光寺街道]]が通る上ノ山の山際を通っていた頃、道脇に水が沸いていた。あまりにも冷たく、一杯で喉が潤い、「一杯清水」と言われる。また、[[盗賊]]が一杯清水の脇に潜んでいて、旅人が2杯目の水を飲もうとすると持ち物を盗む為、1杯しか飲めなく、「一杯清水」になったともいわれている。
==文化財== === 重要文化財（国指定） === *阿弥陀堂 - 重要文化財。明治39年（1906年）4月14日、[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定。 === 新潟県指定文化財 === *鰐口 - 有形文化財（工芸品）昭和57年（1982年）3月26日指定。「永享九年十一月十五日」の銘がある。 *『大般若経』559帖 - 有形文化財（書跡典籍）、平成13年（2001年）3月23日指定。 === 小千谷市指定文化財 === *神輿 - 有形文化財（建造物）。天井板に「明応四年乙卯年（[[1495年]]）四月」という記述がある。しかし、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（[[1817年]]）の修理の際、屋根が当初の様式を失ってしまった。屋根の上には[[鳳凰]]が飾られている。高さ1.8m、重量150kg。昭和47年（1972年）4月1日指定。 *『魚沼神社年中行事記』 - 有形文化財（書跡）、昭和47年4月1日指定。
==参考文献== *五十嵐秀太郎『小千谷の伝説』、[[恒文社]]、昭和60年 *[[小田島允武]]『[[越後野志]]』（翻刻版）、[[歴史図書社]]、昭和49年 *小千谷市文化財協会編『年表小千谷』、小千谷市文化財協会、昭和59年（1984年） *小千谷の歴史編集委員会編『小千谷の歴史』小千谷市教育委員会、2007年。
==関連項目== *[[彌彦神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html 魚沼神社・阿弥陀堂]（小千谷市市役所）
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うおぬましんしや}} [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧県社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:新潟県の重要文化財]] [[Category:小千谷市の歴史]] [[Category:小千谷市の建築物]]

==伝説== ===上杉謙信と神軍=== 上杉謙信が[[関東]]へ出陣する際は、[[小千谷市|小千谷]]で[[信濃川]]を渡っていた。その小千谷を通るたびに、この魚沼神社に参りに来ていた。ある時、[[社殿]]で戦勝祈願の[[儀式]]を済ませて[[拝殿]]に立つと、遥か彼方に[[天香語山命]]と18末社の神々が関東に向かっている姿が見えた。関東の戦いの最中にも神の御旗が現れ、大勝利を抑えた。[[越後国]]に帰ると、謙信は本社に100余貫文の年貢の上がる土地、18末社にもそれぞれ1町8反の土地を寄進した。 ===神に救われた兵=== [[第二次世界大戦]]中、中国大陸を転戦している軍曹がいた。暗闇の夜に、部隊の一番後ろを歩いていると、疲れでよろめいた拍子に道脇の空井戸に落ちてしまった。すると、上から明かりが射し、白い着物の白髪の老人が現れ、提灯を掲げ、縄を下ろされた。井戸から出ると、老人は「ついて来なさい」と言い、歩き出した。道を照らす提灯には「上弥彦神社」と書かれていた。部隊が居る所に着くと、老人はいなくなっていた。 ===婆っ貝=== 信心深いお婆さんがいた。親類の婆さんが長患いで寝ているのを気の毒に、毎日、魚沼神社へ早く治るように祈りに来ていた。ある夜、夢に白装束の神が現れ、「毎日、神社の後ろの田から婆っ貝（[[カラスガイ]]のこと）を採り、親類の婆さんに食べさせれば病気は治る」と言った。次の日から婆っ貝を採って親類の婆さんに届けた。すると、一月ほどで婆さんは病気が治り、前よりも元気になった。 ===大木様=== 神社の倉庫の裏に「大木さま」という大きな[[杉]]の木があった。老木になり、嵐で倒れてしまうと、木の先が上ノ山の麓まで届き橋の様になった。その木で神主は神像を作った。神社の裏にも「大木さま」に負けないほどの大木があった。この木が倒れて、先がとどいた所はいまでも小字で杉崎という地名で残っている。 ===一杯清水=== [[善光寺街道]]が通る上ノ山の山際を通っていた頃、道脇に水が沸いていた。あまりにも冷たく、一杯で喉が潤い、「一杯清水」と言われる。また、[[盗賊]]が一杯清水の脇に潜んでいて、旅人が2杯目の水を飲もうとすると持ち物を盗む為、1杯しか飲めなく、「一杯清水」になったともいわれている。
==文化財== === 重要文化財（国指定） === *阿弥陀堂 - 重要文化財。明治39年（1906年）4月14日、[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定。 === 新潟県指定文化財 === *鰐口 - 有形文化財（工芸品）昭和57年（1982年）3月26日指定。「永享九年十一月十五日」の銘がある。 *『大般若経』559帖 - 有形文化財（書跡典籍）、平成13年（2001年）3月23日指定。 === 小千谷市指定文化財 === *神輿 - 有形文化財（建造物）。天井板に「明応四年乙卯年（[[1495年]]）四月」という記述がある。しかし、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（[[1817年]]）の修理の際、屋根が当初の様式を失ってしまった。屋根の上には[[鳳凰]]が飾られている。高さ1.8m、重量150kg。昭和47年（1972年）4月1日指定。 *『魚沼神社年中行事記』 - 有形文化財（書跡）、昭和47年4月1日指定。
==参考文献== *五十嵐秀太郎『小千谷の伝説』、[[恒文社]]、昭和60年 *[[小田島允武]]『[[越後野志]]』（翻刻版）、[[歴史図書社]]、昭和49年 *小千谷市文化財協会編『年表小千谷』、小千谷市文化財協会、昭和59年（1984年） *小千谷の歴史編集委員会編『小千谷の歴史』小千谷市教育委員会、2007年。
==関連項目== *[[彌彦神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html 魚沼神社・阿弥陀堂]（小千谷市市役所）

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 魚沼神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|300px]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]土川2丁目699-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|18|18.0|N|138|47|15.8|E|region:JP-15_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=魚沼神社}} |祭神 = 天香語山命 |社格 = 式内社論社・伝越後国二宮・旧県社 |創建 = 伝崇神天皇朝 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = 上弥彦大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月7日]] |神事 = }}
'''魚沼神社'''（うおぬまじんじゃ）は[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]土川2丁目にある[[神社]]である。境内には、国の[[重要文化財]]の阿弥陀堂がある。
==祭神== *[[天香語山命]] *[[豊玉姫命]] *阿彦
==歴史== 創建は[[崇神天皇]]の時代と伝えられている。魚沼神社という名は[[平安時代]]からあり、『[[延喜式神名帳|延喜神名式]]』にも[[魚沼郡]]の神社5社が記され、その中に「魚沼神社」もあるが、当神社と一致するか否かについては[[江戸時代]]以来諸説がある。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には、[[彌彦神社]]（[[弥彦村]]）と祭神が同じことから「上弥彦神社」と呼ばれ、[[上杉謙信]]の時代の史料にも「上弥彦大明神」とあり、拝殿にも「彌彦大明神」の額が掲げられている。[[一宮|越後国一宮]]である弥彦村の彌彦神社に対し、当神社を「二の宮」と呼び、この地域の人々の[[信仰]]の中心となった。「魚沼神社」と称したのは[[幕末]]頃で、[[式内社]]であるという確証はない。現在の土川に移る前は上ノ山にあった。
*[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）-魚沼神社が[[従五位下]]の[[神階]]を賜う。 *[[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）-[[新田義貞]]挙兵に伴い魚沼神社神官挙げて従う。 *[[延元]]2年（[[1337年]]）-新田義貞敗れ、従軍した神官は散り、[[慈眼寺]]･円明寺は[[五智院 (小千谷市)|五智院]]末寺となる。 *[[永享]]9年（[[1437年]]）-11月15日、[[西片弥三郎光行]]が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[明応]]元年（[[1492年]]）-西片弥三郎光行が[[懸仏]]4体寄進。 *[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）-9月13日、上杉謙信から社領100貫と魚沼神社18末社に各御供田一町半反を寄進。 *同年-11月28日、上杉謙信[[関東]]出陣の際、魚沼神社に宿泊。 *同12年（[[1569年]]）-[[越中国]][[砺波郡]][[糸岡庄]]七社明神から手写『[[大般若経]]』を魚沼神社へ寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）-神像（現存11体）作成。 *同8年（[[1580年]]）-前々の如く諸役停止し、社領安堵。 *同9年（[[1581年]]）-[[上杉景勝]]から修造料に充てる為に、河舟1艘、関銭免除。 *[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）-玉橋･垣製作 *[[慶長]]3年（[[1598年]]）-[[堀氏]]により、社領を802[[石 (単位)|石]]から10石に削減。 *[[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）-[[朱印地]]20石で紛争。魚沼神社側が有利に決着する。 *[[貞享]]4年（[[1687年]]）-[[上田銀山]]の[[高田藩]]主献上分を魚沼神社で祈祷した上で献上するようになる。 *[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）-[[郷社]]に列し、後に[[県社]]となった。
==境内== [[鳥居]]は、[[両部鳥居]]。[[手水舎]]の隣には、小川の横に御手洗場と見られる石段がある。[[拝殿]]に向かって左側に[[阿弥陀堂]]、右側に[[神楽殿]]、右斜め前には日露戦役記念碑が建てられている。[[本殿]]の脇には、神輿舎、祠がある。
;阿弥陀堂 :[[室町時代]]末期[[永禄]]6年（[[1563年]]）頃の建立で、国の重要文化財に指定されている。方三間、[[宝形造]][[茅葺]]の小堂である。堂内には鍛冶屋が[[信濃川]]の[[砂鉄]]でつくったといわれる[[阿弥陀如来]]像と[[大日如来]]像が安置される。[[昭和]]29年（[[1954年]]）に解体修理が行われ、後世の改変部分は建立当初の形態に復元された。
== 境外末社 == 主に茶郷川流域に18末社がある。 *土川村（現土川） **所謂、天王社 *小千谷町（現本町･元町･平成など） **二荒社 **山王社 **赤指社 *時水村（現時水） **俣倉社 **熊野社 **石動社 **十二社 *藪川村（現藪川） **宇都宮神社 ***永享10年（[[1438年]]）7月19日に西片弥三郎光行が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 **諏訪神社 *吉谷村（現吉谷） **白山社 **五霊社 **若宮社 **十二社 **八幡社 **白山社 *四ツ子村（現四ツ子） **俣倉社 *谷内村（現谷内） **熊野社
==伝説== ===上杉謙信と神軍=== 上杉謙信が[[関東]]へ出陣する際は、[[小千谷市|小千谷]]で[[信濃川]]を渡っていた。その小千谷を通るたびに、この魚沼神社に参りに来ていた。ある時、[[社殿]]で戦勝祈願の[[儀式]]を済ませて[[拝殿]]に立つと、遥か彼方に[[天香語山命]]と18末社の神々が関東に向かっている姿が見えた。関東の戦いの最中にも神の御旗が現れ、大勝利を抑えた。[[越後国]]に帰ると、謙信は本社に100余貫文の年貢の上がる土地、18末社にもそれぞれ1町8反の土地を寄進した。 ===神に救われた兵=== [[第二次世界大戦]]中、中国大陸を転戦している軍曹がいた。暗闇の夜に、部隊の一番後ろを歩いていると、疲れでよろめいた拍子に道脇の空井戸に落ちてしまった。すると、上から明かりが射し、白い着物の白髪の老人が現れ、提灯を掲げ、縄を下ろされた。井戸から出ると、老人は「ついて来なさい」と言い、歩き出した。道を照らす提灯には「上弥彦神社」と書かれていた。部隊が居る所に着くと、老人はいなくなっていた。 ===婆っ貝=== 信心深いお婆さんがいた。親類の婆さんが長患いで寝ているのを気の毒に、毎日、魚沼神社へ早く治るように祈りに来ていた。ある夜、夢に白装束の神が現れ、「毎日、神社の後ろの田から婆っ貝（[[カラスガイ]]のこと）を採り、親類の婆さんに食べさせれば病気は治る」と言った。次の日から婆っ貝を採って親類の婆さんに届けた。すると、一月ほどで婆さんは病気が治り、前よりも元気になった。 ===大木様=== 神社の倉庫の裏に「大木さま」という大きな[[杉]]の木があった。老木になり、嵐で倒れてしまうと、木の先が上ノ山の麓まで届き橋の様になった。その木で神主は神像を作った。神社の裏にも「大木さま」に負けないほどの大木があった。この木が倒れて、先がとどいた所はいまでも小字で杉崎という地名で残っている。 ===一杯清水=== [[善光寺街道]]が通る上ノ山の山際を通っていた頃、道脇に水が沸いていた。あまりにも冷たく、一杯で喉が潤い、「一杯清水」と言われる。また、[[盗賊]]が一杯清水の脇に潜んでいて、旅人が2杯目の水を飲もうとすると持ち物を盗む為、1杯しか飲めなく、「一杯清水」になったともいわれている。
==文化財== === 重要文化財（国指定） === *阿弥陀堂 - 重要文化財。明治39年（1906年）4月14日、[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定。 === 新潟県指定文化財 === *鰐口 - 有形文化財（工芸品）昭和57年（1982年）3月26日指定。「永享九年十一月十五日」の銘がある。 *『大般若経』559帖 - 有形文化財（書跡典籍）、平成13年（2001年）3月23日指定。 === 小千谷市指定文化財 === *神輿 - 有形文化財（建造物）。天井板に「明応四年乙卯年（[[1495年]]）四月」という記述がある。しかし、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（[[1817年]]）の修理の際、屋根が当初の様式を失ってしまった。屋根の上には[[鳳凰]]が飾られている。高さ1.8m、重量150kg。昭和47年（1972年）4月1日指定。 *『魚沼神社年中行事記』 - 有形文化財（書跡）、昭和47年4月1日指定。
==参考文献== {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} *五十嵐秀太郎『小千谷の伝説』、[[恒文社]]、昭和60年 *[[小田島允武]]『[[越後野志]]』（翻刻版）、[[歴史図書社]]、昭和49年 *小千谷市文化財協会編『年表小千谷』、小千谷市文化財協会、昭和59年（1984年） *小千谷の歴史編集委員会編『小千谷の歴史』小千谷市教育委員会、2007年。
==関連項目== *[[彌彦神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html 魚沼神社・阿弥陀堂] - 小千谷市公式ウェブサイト * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 にいがた観光ナビ：魚沼神社阿弥陀堂] - 新潟県観光協会

*[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）-魚沼神社が[[従五位下]]の[[神階]]を賜う。 *[[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）-[[新田義貞]]挙兵に伴い魚沼神社神官挙げて従う。 *[[延元]]2年（[[1337年]]）-新田義貞敗れ、従軍した神官は散り、[[慈眼寺]]･円明寺は[[五智院 (小千谷市)|五智院]]末寺となる。 *[[永享]]9年（[[1437年]]）-11月15日、[[西片弥三郎光行]]が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[明応]]元年（[[1492年]]）-西片弥三郎光行が[[懸仏]]4体寄進。 *[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）-9月13日、上杉謙信から社領100貫と魚沼神社18末社に各御供田一町半反を寄進。 *同年-11月28日、上杉謙信[[関東]]出陣の際、魚沼神社に宿泊。 *同12年（[[1569年]]）-[[越中国]][[砺波郡]][[糸岡庄]]七社明神から手写『[[大般若経]]』を魚沼神社へ寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）-神像（現存11体）作成。 *同8年（[[1580年]]）-前々の如く諸役停止し、社領安堵。 *同9年（[[1581年]]）-[[上杉景勝]]から修造料に充てる為に、河舟1艘、関銭免除。 *[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）-玉橋･垣製作 *[[慶長]]3年（[[1598年]]）-[[堀氏]]により、社領を802[[石 (単位)|石]]から10石に削減。 *[[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）-[[朱印地]]20石で紛争。魚沼神社側が有利に決着する。 *[[貞享]]4年（[[1687年]]）-[[上田銀山]]の[[高田藩]]主献上分を魚沼神社で祈祷した上で献上するようになる。 *[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）-[[郷社]]に列し、後に[[県社]]となった。
==境内== [[鳥居]]は、[[両部鳥居]]。[[手水舎]]の隣には、小川の横に御手洗場と見られる石段がある。[[拝殿]]に向かって左側に[[阿弥陀堂]]、右側に[[神楽殿]]、右斜め前には日露戦役記念碑が建てられている。[[本殿]]の脇には、神輿舎、祠がある。
;阿弥陀堂 :[[室町時代]]末期[[永禄]]6年（[[1563年]]）頃の建立で、国の重要文化財に指定されている。方三間、[[宝形造]][[茅葺]]の小堂である。堂内には鍛冶屋が[[信濃川]]の[[砂鉄]]でつくったといわれる[[阿弥陀如来]]像と[[大日如来]]像が安置される。[[昭和]]29年（[[1954年]]）に解体修理が行われ、後世の改変部分は建立当初の形態に復元された。
== 境外末社 == 主に茶郷川流域に18末社がある。 *土川村（現土川） **所謂、天王社 *小千谷町（現本町･元町･平成など） **二荒社 **山王社 **赤指社 *時水村（現時水） **俣倉社 **熊野社 **石動社 **十二社 *藪川村（現藪川） **宇都宮神社 ***永享10年（[[1438年]]）7月19日に西片弥三郎光行が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 **諏訪神社 *吉谷村（現吉谷） **白山社 **五霊社 **若宮社 **十二社 **八幡社 **白山社 *四ツ子村（現四ツ子） **俣倉社 *谷内村（現谷内） **熊野社
==伝説== ===上杉謙信と神軍=== 上杉謙信が[[関東]]へ出陣する際は、[[小千谷市|小千谷]]で[[信濃川]]を渡っていた。その小千谷を通るたびに、この魚沼神社に参りに来ていた。ある時、[[社殿]]で戦勝祈願の[[儀式]]を済ませて[[拝殿]]に立つと、遥か彼方に[[天香語山命]]と18末社の神々が関東に向かっている姿が見えた。関東の戦いの最中にも神の御旗が現れ、大勝利を抑えた。[[越後国]]に帰ると、謙信は本社に100余貫文の年貢の上がる土地、18末社にもそれぞれ1町8反の土地を寄進した。 ===神に救われた兵=== [[第二次世界大戦]]中、中国大陸を転戦している軍曹がいた。暗闇の夜に、部隊の一番後ろを歩いていると、疲れでよろめいた拍子に道脇の空井戸に落ちてしまった。すると、上から明かりが射し、白い着物の白髪の老人が現れ、提灯を掲げ、縄を下ろされた。井戸から出ると、老人は「ついて来なさい」と言い、歩き出した。道を照らす提灯には「上弥彦神社」と書かれていた。部隊が居る所に着くと、老人はいなくなっていた。 ===婆っ貝=== 信心深いお婆さんがいた。親類の婆さんが長患いで寝ているのを気の毒に、毎日、魚沼神社へ早く治るように祈りに来ていた。ある夜、夢に白装束の神が現れ、「毎日、神社の後ろの田から婆っ貝（[[カラスガイ]]のこと）を採り、親類の婆さんに食べさせれば病気は治る」と言った。次の日から婆っ貝を採って親類の婆さんに届けた。すると、一月ほどで婆さんは病気が治り、前よりも元気になった。 ===大木様=== 神社の倉庫の裏に「大木さま」という大きな[[杉]]の木があった。老木になり、嵐で倒れてしまうと、木の先が上ノ山の麓まで届き橋の様になった。その木で神主は神像を作った。神社の裏にも「大木さま」に負けないほどの大木があった。この木が倒れて、先がとどいた所はいまでも小字で杉崎という地名で残っている。 ===一杯清水=== [[善光寺街道]]が通る上ノ山の山際を通っていた頃、道脇に水が沸いていた。あまりにも冷たく、一杯で喉が潤い、「一杯清水」と言われる。また、[[盗賊]]が一杯清水の脇に潜んでいて、旅人が2杯目の水を飲もうとすると持ち物を盗む為、1杯しか飲めなく、「一杯清水」になったともいわれている。
==文化財== === 重要文化財（国指定） === *阿弥陀堂 - 重要文化財。明治39年（1906年）4月14日、[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定。 === 新潟県指定文化財 === *鰐口 - 有形文化財（工芸品）昭和57年（1982年）3月26日指定。「永享九年十一月十五日」の銘がある。 *『大般若経』559帖 - 有形文化財（書跡典籍）、平成13年（2001年）3月23日指定。 === 小千谷市指定文化財 === *神輿 - 有形文化財（建造物）。天井板に「明応四年乙卯年（[[1495年]]）四月」という記述がある。しかし、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（[[1817年]]）の修理の際、屋根が当初の様式を失ってしまった。屋根の上には[[鳳凰]]が飾られている。高さ1.8m、重量150kg。昭和47年（1972年）4月1日指定。 *『魚沼神社年中行事記』 - 有形文化財（書跡）、昭和47年4月1日指定。
==参考文献== {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} *五十嵐秀太郎『小千谷の伝説』、[[恒文社]]、昭和60年 *[[小田島允武]]『[[越後野志]]』（翻刻版）、[[歴史図書社]]、昭和49年 *小千谷市文化財協会編『年表小千谷』、小千谷市文化財協会、昭和59年（1984年） *小千谷の歴史編集委員会編『小千谷の歴史』小千谷市教育委員会、2007年。
==関連項目== *[[彌彦神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html 魚沼神社・阿弥陀堂] - 小千谷市公式ウェブサイト * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 にいがた観光ナビ：魚沼神社阿弥陀堂] - 新潟県観光協会

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''魚沼神社'''（うおぬまじんじゃ）は[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]土川2丁目にある[[神社]]である。境内には、国の[[重要文化財]]の阿弥陀堂がある。 ==祭神== *[[天香語山命]] *[[豊玉姫命]] *阿彦 ==歴史== 創建は[[崇神天皇]]の時代と伝えられている。魚沼神社という名は[[平安時代]]からあり、『[[延喜式神名帳|延喜神名式]]』にも[[魚沼郡]]の神社5社が記され、その中に「魚沼神社」もあるが、当神社と一致するか否かについては[[江戸時代]]以来諸説がある。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には、[[彌彦神社]]（[[弥彦村]]）と祭神が同じことから「上弥彦神社」と呼ばれ、[[上杉謙信]]の時代の史料にも「上弥彦大明神」とあり、拝殿にも「彌彦大明神」の額が掲げられている。[[一宮|越後国一宮]]である弥彦村の彌彦神社に対し、当神社を「二の宮」と呼び、この地域の人々の[[信仰]]の中心となった。「魚沼神社」と称したのは[[幕末]]頃で、[[式内社]]であるという確証はない。現在の土川に移る前は上ノ山にあった。 *[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）-魚沼神社が[[従五位下]]の[[神階]]を賜う。 *[[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）-[[新田義貞]]挙兵に伴い魚沼神社神官挙げて従う。 *[[延元]]2年（[[1337年]]）-新田義貞敗れ、従軍した神官は散り、[[慈眼寺]]･円明寺は[[五智院 (小千谷市)|五智院]]末寺となる。 *[[永享]]9年（[[1437年]]）-11月15日、[[西片弥三郎光行]]が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[明応]]元年（[[1492年]]）-西片弥三郎光行が[[懸仏]]4体寄進。 *[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）-9月13日、上杉謙信から社領100貫と魚沼神社18末社に各御供田一町半反を寄進。 *同年-11月28日、上杉謙信[[関東]]出陣の際、魚沼神社に宿泊。 *同12年（[[1569年]]）-[[越中国]][[砺波郡]][[糸岡庄]]七社明神から手写『[[大般若経]]』を魚沼神社へ寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 *[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）-神像（現存11体）作成。 *同8年（[[1580年]]）-前々の如く諸役停止し、社領安堵。 *同9年（[[1581年]]）-[[上杉景勝]]から修造料に充てる為に、河舟1艘、関銭免除。 *[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）-玉橋･垣製作 *[[慶長]]3年（[[1598年]]）-[[堀氏]]により、社領を802[[石 (単位)|石]]から10石に削減。 *[[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）-[[朱印地]]20石で紛争。魚沼神社側が有利に決着する。 *[[貞享]]4年（[[1687年]]）-[[上田銀山]]の[[高田藩]]主献上分を魚沼神社で祈祷した上で献上するようになる。 *[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）-[[郷社]]に列し、後に[[県社]]となった。 ==境内== [[鳥居]]は、[[両部鳥居]]。[[手水舎]]の隣には、小川の横に御手洗場と見られる石段がある。[[拝殿]]に向かって左側に[[阿弥陀堂]]、右側に[[神楽殿]]、右斜め前には日露戦役記念碑が建てられている。[[本殿]]の脇には、神輿舎、祠がある。 ;阿弥陀堂 :[[室町時代]]末期[[永禄]]6年（[[1563年]]）頃の建立で、国の重要文化財に指定されている。方三間、[[宝形造]][[茅葺]]の小堂である。堂内には鍛冶屋が[[信濃川]]の[[砂鉄]]でつくったといわれる[[阿弥陀如来]]像と[[大日如来]]像が安置される。[[昭和]]29年（[[1954年]]）に解体修理が行われ、後世の改変部分は建立当初の形態に復元された。 == 境外末社 == 主に茶郷川流域に18末社がある。 *土川村（現土川） **所謂、天王社 *小千谷町（現本町･元町･平成など） **二荒社 **山王社 **赤指社 *時水村（現時水） **俣倉社 **熊野社 **石動社 **十二社 *藪川村（現藪川） **宇都宮神社 ***永享10年（[[1438年]]）7月19日に西片弥三郎光行が鰐口を寄進（新潟県指定文化財）。 **諏訪神社 *吉谷村（現吉谷） **白山社 **五霊社 **若宮社 **十二社 **八幡社 **白山社 *四ツ子村（現四ツ子） **俣倉社 *谷内村（現谷内） **熊野社 ==伝説== ===上杉謙信と神軍=== 上杉謙信が[[関東]]へ出陣する際は、[[小千谷市|小千谷]]で[[信濃川]]を渡っていた。その小千谷を通るたびに、この魚沼神社に参りに来ていた。ある時、[[社殿]]で戦勝祈願の[[儀式]]を済ませて[[拝殿]]に立つと、遥か彼方に[[天香語山命]]と18末社の神々が関東に向かっている姿が見えた。関東の戦いの最中にも神の御旗が現れ、大勝利を抑えた。[[越後国]]に帰ると、謙信は本社に100余貫文の年貢の上がる土地、18末社にもそれぞれ1町8反の土地を寄進した。 ===神に救われた兵=== [[第二次世界大戦]]中、中国大陸を転戦している軍曹がいた。暗闇の夜に、部隊の一番後ろを歩いていると、疲れでよろめいた拍子に道脇の空井戸に落ちてしまった。すると、上から明かりが射し、白い着物の白髪の老人が現れ、提灯を掲げ、縄を下ろされた。井戸から出ると、老人は「ついて来なさい」と言い、歩き出した。道を照らす提灯には「上弥彦神社」と書かれていた。部隊が居る所に着くと、老人はいなくなっていた。 ===婆っ貝=== 信心深いお婆さんがいた。親類の婆さんが長患いで寝ているのを気の毒に、毎日、魚沼神社へ早く治るように祈りに来ていた。ある夜、夢に白装束の神が現れ、「毎日、神社の後ろの田から婆っ貝（[[カラスガイ]]のこと）を採り、親類の婆さんに食べさせれば病気は治る」と言った。次の日から婆っ貝を採って親類の婆さんに届けた。すると、一月ほどで婆さんは病気が治り、前よりも元気になった。 ===大木様=== 神社の倉庫の裏に「大木さま」という大きな[[杉]]の木があった。老木になり、嵐で倒れてしまうと、木の先が上ノ山の麓まで届き橋の様になった。その木で神主は神像を作った。神社の裏にも「大木さま」に負けないほどの大木があった。この木が倒れて、先がとどいた所はいまでも小字で杉崎という地名で残っている。 ===一杯清水=== 重要文化財。明治39年（1906年）4月14日、[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく特別保護建造物（現行法の[[重要文化財]]に相当）に指定。 === 新潟県指定文化財 === *鰐口 - 有形文化財（工芸品）昭和57年（1982年）3月26日指定。「永享九年十一月十五日」の銘がある。 *『大般若経』559帖 - 有形文化財（書跡典籍）、平成13年（2001年）3月23日指定。 === 小千谷市指定文化財 === *神輿 - 有形文化財（建造物）。天井板に「明応四年乙卯年（[[1495年]]）四月」という記述がある。しかし、[[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（[[1817年]]）の修理の際、屋根が当初の様式を失ってしまった。屋根の上には[[鳳凰]]が飾られている。高さ1.8m、重量150kg。昭和47年（1972年）4月1日指定。 *『魚沼神社年中行事記』 - 有形文化財（書跡）、昭和47年4月1日指定。 ==参考文献== {{参照方法|date=2020-03|section=1}} *五十嵐秀太郎『小千谷の伝説』、[[恒文社]]、昭和60年 *[[小田島允武]]『[[越後野志]]』（翻刻版）、[[歴史図書社]]、昭和49年 *小千谷市文化財協会編『年表小千谷』、小千谷市文化財協会、昭和59年（1984年） {{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | Image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | Founded = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]]

Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[category:new pages]][[ja:魚沼神社]]

The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | Image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | Founded = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | Founded = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | Founded = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUonuma Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUonuma Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uonuma Shrine}}[[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | Founded = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Uonuma Shrine}}[[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Uonuma Shrine | image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecrated = Traditionally during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[Amida Nyorai]] and [[Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[1495 CE]] and restored in [[1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]

Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:魚沼神社]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Uonuma Shrine}}[[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:魚沼神社]]
