Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}}

It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]==Notes==

revision.text:
[[ja:魚沼神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] {{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. revision.text:
[[ja:魚沼神社]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] {{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] {{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. revision.text:
[[ja:魚沼神社]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Uonuma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Uonuma Shrine.JPG|270px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Niigata Prefecture]][[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]] Tsuchikawa 2-chōme 699-1 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (disputed), Traditional Second Shrine of [[Echigo Province]], Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | consecration_year = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Main building = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[September 7]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|label=Uonuma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uonuma Shrine''' (魚沼神社, ''Uonuma Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], [[Niigata Prefecture]]. The shrine grounds include the nationally designated Important Cultural Property, the Amida Hall. == Enshrined deities == * [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] * [[Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto]] * Ahi-no-kami (Ahiko) == History == The shrine's foundation is traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The name "Uonuma Shrine" appears in documents from the [[Heian period]] and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' among the five shrines in [[Uonuma District]]. However, its identification as the same Uonuma Shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' has been debated since the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], due to its shared deity with [[Yahiko Shrine]] in [[Yahiko, Niigata|Yahiko]], it was referred to as "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Historical documents from the era of [[Uesugi Kenshin]] mention it as "Kami-Yahiko Daimyōjin," and a plaque reading "Yahiko Daimyōjin" is displayed in the worship hall. The shrine served as a religious center for the region and was later called "Uonuma Shrine" during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period. It was relocated to its current location in Tsuchikawa from the earlier site at Kaminoyama. Key historical milestones include: * [[Jōgan 3]] (861 CE): Uonuma Shrine received the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Genkō 3]] (1333 CE): Shrine priests supported [[Nitta Yoshisada]] during his uprising. * [[Eikyō 9]] (1437 CE): The shrine received a large gong donated by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki (now a Niigata Prefecture-designated cultural property). * [[Eiroku 5]] (1562 CE): [[Uesugi Kenshin]] donated shrine lands and offerings for 18 branch shrines. * [[Eiroku 12]] (1569 CE): A hand-copied version of the ''[[Daihannya-kyō]]'' was donated to the shrine. * [[Meiji 6]] (1873 CE): Designated as a [[Gōsha]] and later promoted to [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Shrine grounds == The main shrine gate is a [[Ryōbu torii]]. Adjacent to the purification fountain, stone steps lead to a historical water purification site. The shrine grounds include the Amida Hall to the left, the Kagura Hall to the right, and a monument commemorating the Russo-Japanese War nearby. ; Amida Hall The Amida Hall, constructed in [[:en:1563 CE|1563 CE]] during the [[Muromachi period]], is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. It is a modest hōgyō-zukuri (hipped roof) structure with thatched roofing. Inside are statues of [[:en:Amida Nyorai|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]], said to have been crafted from iron sand sourced from the [[Shinano River]]. The hall was restored in [[:en:1954 CE|1954 CE]] to its original form. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Amida Hall - Designated under the former Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act in [[:en:1906 CE|1906 CE]]. === Niigata Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Large Gong - Crafted in [[:en:1437 CE|1437 CE]] with inscriptions by Saikata Yasaburō Mitsuyuki. * ''Daihannya-kyō'' (559 scrolls) - Donated in [[:en:1569 CE|1569 CE]]. === Ojiya City-Designated Cultural Properties === * Mikoshi - A portable shrine featuring a phoenix on its roof, originally crafted in [[:en:1495 CE|1495 CE]] and restored in [[:en:1817 CE|1817 CE]]. * ''Uonuma Shrine Annual Event Record'' - Historical documents outlining shrine activities. == Legends == === Uesugi Kenshin and the Divine Army === Uesugi Kenshin is said to have visited the shrine to pray for victory before crossing the [[Shinano River]] en route to the [[Kantō region]]. During one such visit, he reportedly saw a vision of [[Ame-no-Kaguyama]] and other deities marching toward Kantō. The vision inspired his troops and contributed to their subsequent victories. === The Rescued Soldier === During [[World War II]], a soldier who had fallen into a dry well was rescued by a white-haired elderly man holding a lantern marked with "Kami-Yahiko Shrine." Upon being reunited with his unit, the elderly man disappeared. === The Giant Cedar === A massive cedar tree known as "Ōki-sama" stood on the shrine grounds until it was toppled by a storm. Its trunk reportedly stretched down the mountain, serving as a makeshift bridge. == References == * Igarashi Shūtarō, ''Legends of Ojiya,'' Kōbunsha, 1985. * Ojiya City Historical Committee, ''Chronicle of Ojiya,'' Ojiya City Cultural Property Association, 1984. * Ojiya City Board of Education, ''History of Ojiya,'' 2007. == External links == * [http://www.city.ojiya.niigata.jp/soshiki/shogai/uonumajinja-amidado.html Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] - Ojiya City Official Website * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/6089 Niigata Tourism Guide: Uonuma Shrine Amida Hall] {{translated page|ja|魚沼神社|version=102256861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Uonuma Shrine]] [[ja:魚沼神社]] [[sn:onuma Shrine]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Uonuma Shrine (Q11674000)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|lt=Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == '''Uonuma Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Uonuma district|ja|魚沼郡|WD=Q11674002}} || {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|WD=Q11674000}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|37.305000|138.787722|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182443 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11674000}} ==Notes== [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Ojiya, Niigata]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ojiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Syncretic shrines]]
